entry.title: Re: Is it possible to display Custom Application Logs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hi @Daniii I suggest for you to read the full section of SAP Help related to Application Logs, as well as implementing the code snippets provided there:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/read-application-logs-from-database 

Once you make them work for your scenario. Then you need to implement a Custom Entity with a Class that reads the logs using the logic you created in the step above. I suggest for you to follow next tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-a4c-create-custom-entity.html There are plenty helpful tutorials about Custom Entities, in case you need additional information.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2025-1 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a complete, modular Cloud ERP software designed for every business need – powered by AI and analytics. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can run your mission-critical operations in real time from anywhere, introduce new business models in your industry, and expand globally with a trusted partner.  

An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has begun with the first cycle in 2025. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. 

The projects will be open for customer registration until  March 28, 2025. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects for registration: 

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

Seize this opportunity to make your voices heard – register on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025! If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here. 

Warm regards, 

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Chart of Accounts Optimization: The First Step in Your Cloud-Ready Finance Transformation
entry.content:
Chart of Accounts Optimization: The First Step in Your Cloud-Ready Finance Transformation

Introduction

As businesses embark on their digital transformation journeys, finance leaders often find themselves at a crossroads—how to ensure their financial structures are ready for the cloud. The answer lies in optimizing the Chart of Accounts (CoA) as the first and most crucial step. An inefficient CoA can slow down reporting, complicate compliance, and hinder real-time decision-making.

On the other hand, a well-structured CoA serves as the backbone of financial transparency, automation, and scalability—making it a key enabler of cloud transformation. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud becoming the standard for enterprise finance, businesses must rethink their legacy CoA structures to ensure they are lean, scalable, and intelligent.

In this blog, we’ll explore the need for CoA optimization, introduce the SMART framework, and outline a three-step advisory roadmap to get started.

Why CoA Optimization is the First Step in Moving to the Cloud : Many organizations overlook the importance of CoA in cloud transformation. However, migrating to a cloud-based ERP with an outdated or bloated CoA can lead to inefficiencies rather than improvements.

An unstructured CoA can lead to:

❌Redundant GL accounts, making reporting complex

❌Manual reconciliations, slowing financial closing

❌Non-compliant structures, increasing audit risks

❌Inconsistent CoA across business units, causing mismatches

CoA is the Fundamental for Finance

Here’s why optimizing CoA is a non-negotiable first step:

1. Standardization for Global Operations :

A streamlined CoA eliminates redundant accounts and ensures uniform financial reporting across business units and geographies. In cloud environments, a harmonized CoA reduces complexity in multi-entity reporting.

Before Optimization: • Multiple GL accounts for similar transactions across business units (e.g., 5000 - Sales USA, 6000 - Sales Europe). • Inconsistencies in reporting structures lead to complex financial consolidation.

After Optimization: • Harmonized CoA across all entities (e.g., 5000 - Sales Revenue, with dimensions for country-wise reporting).

• Consolidated reporting becomes automated, reducing reconciliation time by 40%.

Sample CoA Structure

Result: A leaner, standardized CoA allows for real-time reporting, compliance automation, and AI-powered financial insights.

2. Real-Time Financial Insights

A well-structured CoA enables SAP S/4HANA’s Universal Journal, allowing finance teams to get real-time insights instead of batch-processed reports. This is critical for businesses leveraging predictive analytics and AI-driven decision-making.

Before Optimization: • Sales and cost data scattered across multiple accounts, requiring manual adjustments. • No direct visibility into profitability at product or region level.

After Optimization: • Optimized CoA structured for real-time tracking via SAP Universal Journal. • AI-driven insights allow predictive sales analytics, improving demand forecasting.

3. Regulatory Compliance & Audit Readiness: 

Cloud-based finance systems need to comply with multiple regulations (IFRS, GAAP, tax laws). An optimized CoA ensures compliance with built-in financial controls, reducing audit complexities.

Before Optimization: • GL accounts not aligned with IFRS/GAAP, requiring manual intervention during audits. • High risk of non-compliance due to region-specific accounting structures.

After Optimization: • CoA aligned with global and local accounting standards. • AI-based anomaly detection flags potential compliance risks in real-time.

4. Seamless Integration Across Finance & Operations  Finance is no longer an isolated function—it interacts with logistics, sales, HR, and procurement. A clean CoA structure ensures smooth data flow across all modules in SAP, enabling automation and intelligent processing.

Before Optimization: • Finance, logistics, and sales maintained separate CoA structures, causing data mismatches. • Month-end closing delayed due to manual reconciliation efforts.

After Optimization: • Unified CoA across finance and operations, allowing real-time integration with procurement and sales. • AI-powered auto-reconciliation reduces closing time by 50%.

5. AI & Automation Readiness :

 AI-powered financial automation (auto-reconciliation, predictive analytics) depends on clean, structured data. A well-optimized CoA allows machine learning models to operate efficiently, reducing manual effort in financial operations.

Before Optimization: • Legacy CoA with excessive manual processes for financial analysis. • Error-prone data hinders AI adoption for automation.

After Optimization: • AI-optimized CoA structure allows for automated journal entries and predictive financial forecasting. • AI-driven fraud detection reduces financial risks by 20%.

With these factors in mind, organizations must approach CoA optimization with a structured framework.

This is where SMART  comes into play.

Introducing the SMART  Framework for CoA Optimization The SMART framework provides a structured methodology for designing a lean, scalable, and AI-ready CoA for the future.

SMART – Core Optimization for CoA

🔹S – Standardization & Simplification

✅Reduce redundant GL accounts and align CoA structures across entities.

✅Use AI-driven tools to analyze transaction patterns and eliminate unused accounts.

✅Before Optimization: Multiple redundant GL accounts (e.g., 10001 - Advertising USA, 10002 - Advertising UK).

✅After Optimization: One standard GL (1000 - Advertising Expense) with dimensions for country-wise tracking. 📌 Use Case: A global FMCG firm reduced its GL accounts by 40% using AI-based CoA mapping, accelerating financial consolidation.

Journal Entry Example:

🔻Before Optimization

(Legacy CoA with Redundant Accounts):

Dr. 601100 - Print Marketing Expense $10,000

Cr. 200100 - Accounts Payable $10,000

🔹After Optimization

(Standardized CoA with Dimensions):

Dr. 601000 - Marketing Expense (Print) $10,000

Cr. 200100 - Accounts Payable $10,000

🔹M – Modernization for Digital Finance

✅Leverage S/4HANA’s Universal Journal for real-time reporting and insights.

✅Use AI for automated anomaly detection in financial reporting.

✅Before Optimization: Delayed insights due to batch processing of financial data.

✅After Optimization: AI-driven financial reporting with real-time cost tracking.

🔻Before Optimization (Manual Revenue Recognition):

Dr. 120000 - Accounts Receivable $50,000

Cr. 400001 - Sales Revenue USA $50,000

🔹After Optimization (AI-Powered Real-Time CoA with Dimensions):

Dr. 120000 - Accounts Receivable $50,000

Cr. 400000 - Sales Revenue (Region: USA, Product: Electronics) $50,000

📌Use Case: A manufacturing company used AI to flag unusual cost variances, reducing financial errors by 25%.

🔹A – Alignment with Compliance & Regulatory Needs

✅Map CoA to IFRS, GAAP, and tax regulations, ensuring global compliance.

✅Automate regulatory reporting using AI-based compliance monitoring.

✅Before Optimization: Different CoA structures across regions, leading to compliance challenges.

✅After Optimization: AI-powered real-time audit trails and automated regulatory reporting.

Journal Entry Example:

🔻Before Optimization (Manually Adjusted Depreciation Accounting):

Dr. 650010 - Depreciation Expense (Old CoA) $5,000

Cr. 170000 - Accumulated Depreciation $5,000

🔹After Optimization (IFRS-Compliant AI-Driven CoA):

Dr. 650000 - Depreciation Expense (IFRS) $5,000

Cr. 170000 - Accumulated Depreciation $5,000

📌Use Case: A multinational bank reduced compliance efforts by 50% through AI-driven reporting automation.

🔹R – Real-time Insights & Reporting

✅Structure CoA to enable predictive analytics and AI-powered financial forecasting.

✅Use embedded SAP analytics for real-time tracking of business performance.

✅Before Optimization: Fragmented revenue recognition across different CoA structures.

✅After Optimization: AI-powered CoA with real-time revenue forecasting.

Journal Entry Example:

🔻Before Optimization (Manually Categorized Cost of Goods Sold - COGS):

Dr. 500001 - Raw Material Cost $30,000

Dr. 500002 - Labor Cost $20,000

Cr. 210000 - Accounts Payable $50,000

🔹After Optimization (AI-Enhanced COGS Categorization in Universal Journal):

Dr. 500000 - COGS (Material: Steel, Region: APAC) $50,000

Cr. 210000 - Accounts Payable $50,000

📌Use Case: A retail firm leveraged SKU-level revenue tracking to optimize pricing and inventory planning.

🔹T – Tailored for Future Scalability

✅Ensure flexibility for M&A, new business models, and rapid expansions.

✅Use AI-based simulations to test post-merger financial structures.

✅Before Optimization: Rigid CoA structure unable to accommodate new business models.

✅After Optimization: AI-assisted CoA simulations for mergers, acquisitions, and expansions.

Journal Entry Example:

🔻Before Optimization (Different CoA Structures Across Merged Companies):

Dr. 702010 - M&A Costs - Company A $100,000

Dr. 800010 - Acquisition Costs - Company B $100,000

Cr. 100000 - Bank $200,000

🔹After Optimization (Unified CoA for M&A Readiness):

Dr. 700000 - M&A and Acquisition Costs $200,000

Cr. 100000 - Bank $200,000

📌Use Case: A healthcare company reduced M&A integration time by 40% using AI-assisted CoA restructuring.

SRI – Strategic Resilience & Integration with AI

🔹S – System-Wide Integration

✅Harmonize CoA across finance, logistics, and controlling for seamless operations.

✅Use AI-powered transaction matching to reconcile procurement costs.

📌Use Case: A pharma company reduced financial mismatches by 30% using AI-led reconciliation.

🔹R – Risk & Compliance Management

✅Implement AI-based fraud detection for risk-based account classifications

. 📌 Use Case: A telecom firm reduced financial fraud risks by 20% with AI-driven fraud detection models.

🔹I – Intelligent Automation Readiness

✅Enable AI-based auto-reconciliation and predictive financial analysis.

📌Use Case: A tech company reduced manual effort by 70% using AI-powered reconciliation automation.

Three-Step Advisory for CoA Optimization

Assess Your Current CoA Structure - Conduct a CoA health check—identify redundancies, unused accounts, and compliance gaps. Use AI-based tools to analyze historical transactions for pattern-based recommendations.

Design & Align Your Future-Ready CoA - Define the target CoA structure based on Global Best principles. Align with IFRS, GAAP, and tax regulations to ensure compliance. Implement AI-powered GL account mapping and auto-reconciliation features.

Implement & Monitor with AI & Automation - Migrate your optimized CoA to SAP S/4HANA Cloud). Set up real-time financial dashboards for tracking business performance. Enable AI-driven risk analytics and automation for fraud detection and compliance monitoring.

 

Key Takeaways

✔️Optimizing CoA is the foundation for a successful cloud-based finance transformation—without it, your digital initiatives may struggle with inefficiencies.

✔️The framework ensures that CoA is standardized, scalable, and AI-ready.

✔️AI and automation are critical enablers in streamlining financial operations, improving compliance, and accelerating insights.

✔️A structured three-step approach—assess, design, and implement—can make CoA optimization a smooth transition rather than a complex overhaul.

By making CoA optimization your first step, you set the stage for a truly intelligent, agile, and future-ready finance function.

 

Are you ready to take that step? 

Srinath Ganesan

entry.title: Issue with SAP BTP SDK for Android (v24.4.3) After App Termination and Re-launch
entry.content:
Dear Team,

This report details an issue encountered with the SAP BTP SDK for Android, version 24.4.3, integrated into our Android application.

Observed Behavior:

The application successfully authenticates and performs its intended tasks after initial installation and launch. However, upon terminating the application from the background (e.g., swiping it away from the recent apps list) and subsequently re-launching it, the application displays a "Web page not available" error. Attached the screenshot for reference.

Specific Symptoms:

  • The error occurs immediately after re-launching the application from a terminated state.
  • The issue manifests as a web page loading failure, indicative of a potential problem with the SDK's internal web view or authentication state.
  • Navigating back within the application after encountering the error appears to resolve the issue, allowing the application to function correctly.

entry.title: Hurdles / Pitfalls in End-User Training for Software Implementation Programs
entry.content:
We all know that when an organization decides to modernize its legacy systems for new technology, End-User Training becomes critical for the successful transformation. However, several pitfalls / hurdles can undermine the effectiveness of end-user training.

Based on my experience of leading End User Adoption activities for various system implementation programs (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Ariba, Microsoft Dynamics, custom applications and platforms, etc.), I will share the key hurdles to watch out for.

Training Content Not User-centric

We happen to draft end-user training content, which is too technical, too general, or unclear & irrelevant. For example, the end-user training decks just show a dump of some as-is workflow diagrams (prepared by technical team) along with random system screenshots. OR, the end-user training decks have many references to configurations, integrations, middleware, technical system rules, back-end automated activities, etc. Such training content clearly indicates that the content is not user-centric. Such training decks make the understanding of new system even harder for the end users.

To avoid this, we must do careful & quality Instructional Design to generate result-oriented learning experiences for corporate users, for example:

  • Drive the learning experiences by the objectives, drafted around the expected user behaviour linked to the execution of actual business operations in the new system.
  • Focus on real-world scenarios & actual business processes, plus do the required detailing of Concepts, New Terminology, & Processes.
  • Do easier scaffolding of information to draft simple, visual, and well-structured materials tailored to the audience.
  • Drive the content coverage and presentation from the perspective of how adults learn and how their learning psychology works.
  • Generate the focus and reinforcement areas for key learning takeaways throughout the learning experiences.
  • Achieve user-engagement through various creative ways, e.g., content gamification, hands-on experiences, real-life scenarios, etc. to keep the learners hooked to the content and push the required learning.

Remember that if the training sessions are boring or irrelevant, users may lose interest and not absorb the information.

Training Content Not Tailored to Roles

Learning Consultants tend to think of one-size-fits all training, and ignore different roles and skill levels while drafting the content. To avoid this, we can tailor training to account for different user groups or job roles and workflows across departments in the client’s organization. Generally, the end-user training for new system implementation needs to encompass various roles, such as Warehouse workers, Finance teams, Procurement staff, etc.

The role-based training will educate end-users only the features that they need, for example, Accounts Payable (AP) Staff will learn invoice processing, and Finance Managers will learn Reporting tools.

If the same generic training is given to all users, employees will struggle to find relevant information in the training content, and may skip the training sessions or get disengaged. This will eventually increase errors after go-live, causing operational delays.

Poor Planning and Timing for End-User Training

Generally, it is observed that end-user training sessions are scheduled too early, which makes the end-users forget what they have learned before go-live. For example, SAP training was delivered 3-4 months before go-live. By the time employees need to use the system, they forget most of what they learned.

On the other hand, if we conduct training sessions too late, it leads to the rushed sessions before go-live, or we squeeze the training into a short timeframe. This way, we do not allow enough time for hands-on practice and feedback.

Poor planning and timing will lead to the increased support tickets overwhelming the IT Support Team and Functional SAP Consultants. This eventually will drop the productivity as employees will struggle to complete tasks.

As a solution, we can conduct the training closer to go-live, offer refresher sessions post implementation, and provision just-in-time performance support knowledge base.

Inadequate Training Methods

While executing the end-user training sessions, we over-rely on passive training methods, for example, PowerPoint-driven instructor sessions / lectures, instead of interactive learning, or modern learning approaches like micro-learning, gamification, self-paced modules, on-demand videos, etc.

For ideal results, we can creatively use a blended approach, for example, self-paced modules, live demos, hands-on practice, self-help performance support material, etc.

Also, if we do not provision a sandbox environment / interactive hands-on practice for users to practice safely, this leads to poor user-adoption. If the training is more theoretical using PowerPoint presentations, rather than live demonstrations or sandbox environment, end-users will not get the confidence and will struggle with actual transactions during go-live.

Failure to Address Change Resistance

If the end-users do not feel positive about the change and carry the change-resistance in their minds, they will not get engaged in the training experiences, and will not take the training seriously. Hence, the organization should not assume that employees would embrace the new system (e.g., SAP) because the leadership mandated it.

If there is no clear communication on why the change is happening or how it will benefit the users, they will not make serious efforts to acquire the learning. We need to remember that negative attitude from users will make the adoption even harder.

As a solution, we need to implement a change management strategy, with leadership involvement and user champions to drive the adoption. The change management efforts should go hand-in-hand with the end-user adoption efforts.

No Change Champions or Super Users

Organizations treat training as a one-time event rather than ongoing process, and they undermine the need of Change Champions and Super Users to support and reinforce the ongoing learning.

As Learning Consultant, we need to guide the client on the role of Change Champions and Super Users, and how the client can identify & qualify them. For example, Change Champions and Super Users should have strong understanding the system and processes; they should be involved in various implementation activities (e.g., design sessions, system demos, testing, etc.) right from the beginning.

No Post-training Support

Generally, I have observed that once the training is done, users have no-easy way to get help when they face problems. Also, the IT/Helpdesk teams are not prepared to answer the questions.

The post-training support must be in place to ensure the long-term success. If we do not offer follow-up sessions, refresher sessions, on-demand learning materials & support; FAQs, Quick Reference Guides, and ongoing learning opportunities, it will weaken the transfer of learning to real-life. This will eventually overwhelm the Helpdesk with calls and tickets.

Here, as a solution, we can offer ongoing support, including quick reference guides, advanced performance support framework & knowledge base; on-demand assistance by Change Champions and Super Users, dedicated Support Members for tricky SAP modules (e.g., Finance transactions) for troubleshooting; dedicated Help Desk to assist with adoption issues, and so on.

Failure to Measure Training Effectiveness

For training effectiveness, we rely only on attendance and do not assess real knowledge retention and transfer. For example, we do not have a mechanism to measure actual user understanding; no follow-up surveys or feedback loops for improve training, no metrics to track the post-training performance to measure adoption, etc.

As a solution, we can evaluate effectiveness through assessments, surveys, usage metrics, etc.

Conclusion

It is essential to avid the above pitfalls / hurdles to generate the effective end-user training experiences.

entry.title: Batch Job sporadically cancels with error "Object requested is currently locked by user CONTROLM"
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a custom program which updates a field in Table VEKP. The program is run in background mode in Production every 5 mins with user CONTROLM. It usually completes within 2 mins so there is never an overlap of 2 batch jobs of the same program. But, sporadically (5 times out of 120) the batch job fails because of lock issue. 

I tried to check for other batch jobs by CONTROLM which updates VEKP in the same period but didn't find any.

I ran an Enqueue trace in ST12. The trace only shows the program name as "SAPLSENA"  against the Object "EVVEKPE" without fully displaying the ABAP Call Heirarchy.

I do not have access to SM12 in Production yet. Is there a better solution than checking the lock entries while waiting for the batch job to cancel? How do I catch which program run by CONTROLM is causing this issue? 

Best Regards,

Laxman

entry.title: Re: Import on-premise S/4HANA transports into S/4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Currently we don't have a feature that allows transports between S/4HANA on-premise and cloud. However, you can use abapGit.

AbapGit is an open source developed Git client for the ABAP server to import and export ABAP objects between ABAP systems. With abapGit, you can export your ABAP objects from any system to another, typically from on-premise to cloud or from one cloud system to another.

Kindly check the following documentations:

  • SAP Help Portal | Working with abapGit
  • SAP Help Portal | Key User Extensibility and abapGit
  • SAP Help Portal | Import Content from abapGit Repository into the ABAP Environment 

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: How to use the Libraries effectively in SAP Cloud ALM?

entry.title: Is it possible to add SAP systems with the same SID to a SAP Solution Manager system?
entry.content:
How to add multiple SAP systems with the same SID to a single SAP Solution Manager system during a migration from on premise system into the Hyperscaler like Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add SAP systems with the same SID t...
entry.content:
Dear,

Assuming a system XXX is installed on one server, then after a "copy" of the source system to an Azure infrastructure, over a different DB Hostname server, correct? So, this will generate a different system number for this second XXX system.

Following that, by checking the resolution section provided in KBA 3417361:

  • “As SID, Installation Number, and DB Hostname are a system's key entries, please ensure that the key entries (Installation Number & DB Hostname) are not the same. SAP Solution Manager can manage the systems with the same SID in this scenario. An extended SID is automatically generated for the second or the third system to distinguish the difference.”

Here are the steps you need to take:

  1. Ensure that the key entries (Installation Number & DB Hostname) are not the same for the two systems. This is because SID, Installation Number, and DB Hostname are a system's key entries.
  2. SAP Solution Manager can manage the systems with the same SID in this scenario. An extended SID is automatically generated for the second or the third system to distinguish the difference.
  3. At LMDB level, there will be two different technical systems XXX and XXX00001.
  4. ChaRM is using the configuration item of the production system in the change documents. Therefore, only when the production system gets replaced, you will need to take care of changing the configuration item of the production system.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Program to Upload PDF Form to OneDrive
entry.content:
I suppose you are trying to implement it in S4 on premise .
To upload a PDF file to a OneDrive folder using a shared link in ABAP, you can leverage the Microsoft Graph API for OneDrive and the Harmonized Document Management (HDM) concept in S/4HANA. Below is a sample ABAP code snippet to help you get started:

  1. Set up the Microsoft Graph API: You need to register your application in the Azure portal to get the necessary credentials (client ID, tenant ID, and client secret).

  2. ABAP Code: The following code demonstrates how to upload a PDF file to OneDrive using the shared link and HDM.

    DATA: lv_url TYPE string,
      lv_token TYPE string,
      lv_file_path TYPE string,
      lv_file_name TYPE string,
      lv_shared_link TYPE string,
      lv_response TYPE string,
      lv_content TYPE xstring.

" Set your OneDrive shared link and file details
lv_shared_link = 'YOUR_ONEDRIVE_SHARED_LINK'.
lv_file_path = 'YOUR_LOCAL_FILE_PATH'.
lv_file_name = 'YOUR_FILE_NAME.pdf'.

" Get the access token (you need to implement the OAuth 2.0 flow to get the token)
lv_token = 'YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'.

" Read the PDF file content
CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_upload
  EXPORTING
    filename = lv_file_path
    filetype = 'BIN'
  IMPORTING
    filelength = lv_file_length
  CHANGING
    data_tab = lv_content.

" Create the upload URL
lv_url = lv_shared_link && '/content'.

" Make the HTTP request to upload the file
DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_http_client=>create_by_url( lv_url ).
lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'PUT' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Authorization' value = |Bearer { lv_token }| ).
lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/pdf' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_content ).

" Send the request
lo_http_client->send( ).
lo_http_client->receive( ).

" Get the response
lv_response = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

" Check the response
IF lo_http_client->response->get_status( ) = '200'.
  WRITE: / 'File uploaded successfully'.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'File upload failed', lv_response.
ENDIF.

    Note:

    • Replace YOUR_ONEDRIVE_SHARED_LINK, YOUR_LOCAL_FILE_PATH, YOUR_FILE_NAME.pdf, and YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN with your actual values.
    • Implement the OAuth 2.0 flow to get the access token. You can use the cl_http_client class to make the necessary HTTP requests for the OAuth flow.

    This code provides a basic example of how to upload a PDF file to OneDrive using a shared link. You may need to adjust the code to fit your specific requirements and environment.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Customizing of content form template in Output Management
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

To know more about this offering, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution

This blog showcases how the content form template can be customized to change the standard PDF generated by output control in service quotation.

Resource-Related Quotation

The resource-related quotation process begins when an execution order item is added in the service quotation. Once the execution order item is sent to planning by the service manager, it triggers the generation of a maintenance order. The maintenance order contains various operations and components based on planning, which are integral to fulfil the service requirements.

Once the planning is completed by maintenance planner, the planned data on the maintenance order is transferred to the service quotation via the DIP engine in the form of the resource-related cost line items. This are created as the subitems of main execution order item. These lines are distinguished from any other sub-items using the item category usage ‘RRCO’ (Resource-Related Cost).

To understand the process flow in Resource-Related Quotation, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 3 – Planning and Cost Estimation in Service Quotation

Send to Customer

When the service quotation is sent to customer, the execution order item status is updated to ‘Released’. A standard PDF is generated by the output management which shows the details of the service quotation including items. This can be viewed in the Output Control assignment block. However, the resource-related cost items are not displayed in the standard PDF.

What is FDP?

FDP (Form Data Provider) is a gateway model that supplies the necessary data for generating output documents, such as invoices or purchase orders. It acts as the data source for a form template used in the output process, defining the structure and content of the document in the desired format.

What is output form?

Output form is a form which is used to display data in the PDF format to the customer where customer can see the items and subitems with respect to their pricing. 

Output form will read the data from FDP. 

Customizing Output Form 

The output PDF can be customized to enable display of resource-related cost line items as explained below.

Inside the form template, there is a provision to write scripts. The script must be modified to enable the resource-related cost line items in output form. 

The field SrvcQtnItmIsRsceReltdCostItm is already available in the node CRMS4S_SERVICEDOCUMENTITEMNODE for the OData FDP_CRMS4_SERVICE_QUOTATION.

This field value will be true if an item is resource-related cost item with item category usage as RRCO. This field will be consumed in the Script Section of the Adobe Print Form to suppress the RRCO line items in PDF output. The script will be edited using the Adobe Live Cycle Designer.

Step 1: Download the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Downloading layout.
  • An XDP file will be downloaded.

Step 2: Modify the script in Adobe LiveCycle.

  • Open the XDP file in Adobe LiveCycle App.
  • In the Design view, the output format for the PDF can be seen.
  • In the rowTableSection -> rowTableItem, select the colItmCatUsg.
  • Open the Events with Scripts view in the Show option. The RRCO line items are suppressed in the output PDF by following logic. This code can be removed from the script to display the RRCO line items in output PDF.
  • Save the changes to the script.

Step 3: Upload the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Uploading layout
  • Upload the modified XDP file.

After this step, when the service quotation is sent to the customer, the generated PDF will display resource-related cost items as well.

Summary

The standard PDF in the output control can be customized according to business requirement to enable or disable the display of resource-related cost items.

entry.title: Deploy SAP Integration Suite - Edge Integration Cell - on Google Cloud Platform
entry.content:
What is Edge Integration Cell?

Edge Integration Cell (EIC), part of the SAP Integration Suite, brings a powerful hybrid integration runtime that bridges the gap between the cloud and your private landscape. It provides the flexibility to seamlessly manage APIs and run integration scenarios within a controlled environment, combining the best of both worlds.

With its hybrid deployment model, EIC makes it easy to design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while still allowing it to be securely deployed and run in a customer managed private environment.

 

Use cases and Advantages of EIC

  • Security or compliance use cases: Sensitive data stays safely within an enterprise's firewall, following strict rules to keep it within a private environment.
  • Migration path for SAP Process Integration customers: EIC helps customers design and monitor integration content in the cloud, while keeping deployment and execution fully within their private environments.

This blog offers a step-by-step guide to deploying Edge Integration Cell on GCP, with two setup options: a quick setup for trying out the simplest setup and a high-availability configuration for production. Whether you're looking for a quick test or a scalable, production-ready deployment, this guide makes it easy to configure SAP BTP and GCP resources. Once the setup is complete, the SAP Integration Suite can deploy integration flows securely to on-premise setup using EIC.

 

Architecture

Both the quick setup and the high availability (HA) setup architecture for deploying EIC on GCP utilize Google Cloud Kubernetes Engine (GKE). The quick setup uses the default Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) in the region, with all storage handled by GKE’s default storage classes and smaller compute and storage resources for a simple and efficient deployment.

In contrast, the HA architecture is spread across at least three availability zones for redundancy, with dynamic storage managed by the GKE cluster, along with additional services like Cloud SQL PostgreSQL for persistence and Memorystore Redis for caching.

On the SAP BTP side, the Edge Integration Cell must be activated within the SAP Integration Suite. Once the EIC is configured and successfully deployed on the GCP GKE cluster, integration flows can be designed and seamlessly deployed to the new EIC runtime, combining the powerful integration capabilities of SAP Integration Suite with the customer managed resources on GCP.

 

Edge Integration Cell setup overview on SAP BTP and GCP

Here is an outline of the steps involved in setting up and deploying Edge Integration Cell from SAP BTP environment to GCP.

Setup on GCP:

 

 Setup on SAP BTP:

You can find the detailed setup instructions in this GitHub repository.

 

Additional resources

Here are some additional resources

  • What is Edge Integration Cell | SAP Help
  • Deploy Edge Integration Cell on a custom server using SUSE Rancher
  • FAQ on Edge Integration Cell
  • SAP Integration Suite | SAP Help

 

Conclusion

I hope this blog has helped you set up Edge Integration Cell on GCP, making it easier to connect SAP on-premise solutions with the flexibility to design and monitor integrations in the cloud. By following these steps, you can build secure, high-performing business applications that blend innovation with control in a safe environment.

I want to express my gratitude to our SAP team members, Adarsh Hedge, Madan Pichamuthu, and Sivakumar N for their valuable contributions, guidance, and support. If you have any questions, feel free to contact paa@sap.com.

entry.title: Creating a Multi - input and Multi -output model
entry.content:
SAP provides a wonderful service over BTP DAR(Document Attribute Recommender ) which allows multi input and multi output model . However some Financial Companies handles extremely sensitive financial data, including personal information and transaction details.
They might be concerned about data privacy and security and are often subject to strict regulatory requirements regarding data handling and storage. 
In such a Scenario when they do not want to transfer any data over any other platform but make it in house using the following steps.

Let's understand each stage of the code to achieve this feature.

1. Data Preparation

First, you need to prepare your data. Let's assume you have a dataset with both numerical and categorical features.

2. Preprocessing

You need to preprocess your data to convert categorical variables into numerical ones. This is done using encoders like "OneHotEncoder" for categorical features and StandardScaler for numerical features.

Both OneHotEncoder and StandardScaler are available in Python through the scikit-learn library.

You can install scikit-learn using pip 

pip install scikit-learn

3. Model Training

Next, you create a pipeline that includes both the preprocessing steps and the model. This ensures that the same transformations are applied during both training and inference.

4. Model Inference

For inference, you need to preprocess the input data in the same way as during training, make predictions, and then convert the predictions back to their original categorical values.

So here  is the entire coding

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

# Example input data
X_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A', 'B', 'A'],
    'Vendor': ['X', 'Y', 'Z'],
    'Amount': [100, 200, 150],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1', 'Type2', 'Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021, 2022, 2021]
})

# Example output data
y_train = pd.DataFrame({
    'HKONT': ['Account1', 'Account2', 'Account1'],
    'KOSTL': ['Cost1', 'Cost2', 'Cost1'],
    'Profitcenters': ['Center1', 'Center2', 'Center1'],
    'Paymentterms': ['Term1', 'Term2', 'Term1'],
    'Partnerbanktype': ['Bank1', 'Bank2', 'Bank1'],
    'Taxcode': ['Tax1', 'Tax2', 'Tax1']
})

# Preprocessing for numerical and categorical data
preprocessor = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ('num', StandardScaler(), ['Amount', 'Fiscalyear']),
        ('cat', OneHotEncoder(), ['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'InvoiceDocumenttype'])
    ])

# Multi-output model
model = Pipeline(steps=[
    ('preprocessor', preprocessor),
    ('classifier', MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier()))
])

# Fit the model
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Example input for prediction
X_test = pd.DataFrame({
    'Company_code': ['A'],
    'Vendor': ['X'],
    'Amount': [120],
    'InvoiceDocumenttype': ['Type1'],
    'Fiscalyear': [2021]
})

# Predict
predictions = model.predict(X_test)

# Convert predictions to a readable format
fields = ['HKONT', 'KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']
prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, predictions[0]))
print(prediction_dict)

entry.title: API LMS recordLearningEvents
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a constant problem in using this API:

/learning/odatav4/public/admin/learningEvent/v1/recordLearningEvents

documentation was not helpful:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_LEARNING/5aab9bef78fc4c4fa199c1f7aa142720/838be5e30e554350aa5149df626268c4.html

and looking through the metadata I couldn't find any useful information.

My body is as follows:

{
    "recordLearningEvents": [
        {
        "componentTypeID": "02",
        "componentID": "TE70",
        "revisionDate": 1428506077000,
        "completionStatusID": "04",
        "studentID" : "E1545",
        "completionDate": 1428506077000,
        "completionTimeZoneID": "US/Eastern",
        "contactHours":1,
        "totalHours":1
        }
    ]
}
I tried various combinations with various users, courses, componentTypeID or completionStatusID;
but the error continues to be:
"errorMessage": "No items found matching input parameters."
 
Do you know how I can solve it? Or do you know another method to update via API the Learning History?

entry.title: Importing Actuals from ACDOCA table into SAC using API
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to fetch Actuals data for certain expenses from the ACDOCA table in S/4 HANA into SAC. Preferred approach is to use any existing API's to import the required transaction data at Company code, Cost Center, G/L account level. We found the OData service API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV (Journal Entry Item - Read (A2X)) in the help documentation. However this API is not available for the S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition version 1909 in the Business Accelerator Hub.
Is there any other OData service that we can use to import the Actual transaction data from ACDOCA into SAC?
If not an API, what other options are available for meeting our requirement considering there's no SAP BW or Datasphere in the client IT landscape?

Regards,

Manas

entry.title: SAP Inside Track DFW 2025 - Dallas
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community from Dallas - FWT Texas.

We will be hosting the second SAP Inside Track event in DFW at the SAP Plano office location.
This event is a free event with a max capacity of 20 attendees.  

Register here

The main reason for organizing this event is to continue our community awareness within the DFW area, hopefully we can grow it into other cities and perhaps other states.

If you like to learn, network and if you work in S/4HANA, BTP, AI, Fiori, or any other SAP area, then this event is for you. We will also have food and drinks at the event as well. Don't miss the opportunity to participate.  See you there.

 

When:


Thursday, March 27th, 2025  3:00 - 6:00pm CST

 

Where:


SAP Office

7500 Windrose Ave. #250, Plano, TX 75024

Agenda

  1. Business Process Health – Ginger Lutrell
  2. SAP Learning Hub and SAP’s “Get and Stay Certified” Program – there are several recent announcements by SAP Learning designed to improved SAP developer certifications.  Riley Rainey will review the announcements and can answer questions about the new programs you might have.
  3. Experiments Leveraging Agent AI Capabilities inside SAP – recent developments in Agentic AI offer rich new capabilities for all developers.  Riley Rainey leads the Tutorial Subsystem team inside SAP. SAP Tutorial content is used by up to 20,000 developers a month via the SAP Tutorial Navigator. Riley will discuss fresh experiments that SAP is conducting to use agentic AI and LLMs for quality assurance in SAP’s tutorial subsystem.  This talk describes useful tips for using SAP AI Hub and open source resources such as Ollama, and AI agents like the “browser-use” project.
  4. Navigating a Complex Production Upgrade: A Story of Innovation and Adaptation by Surya Chavali
  1. Designing for AI: Moving Beyond Interfaces to Collaboration
    AI is changing the way people interact with technology, but great AI design isn't just about smarter interfaces—it’s about crafting meaningful collaboration between humans and AI. This talk explores key principles for designing AI-powered experiences that empower users, enhance trust, and create real value. (Virtual Presentation by Anshu Tank)

 

Sponsors:

If you or your company would like to help sponsor the event, please contact me directly on the SAP community. Thank you to the already sponsors for the event.

 

 

Thank you all and looking forward to seeing you at the event.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 Public cloud - Service - ATP check on service...
entry.content:
Hello,

ATP (Available to Promise) check functionality is currently not supported for service quotations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The standard process proposed by SAP for handling service quotations in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is as follows:

  1. Create the service quotation manually without ATP check.
  2. If needed, check availability manually while creating the quotation as a workaround.
  3. Once the quotation is accepted, create a service order.
  4. Perform the ATP check in the resulting service order, where the functionality is fully supported.

Regarding future enhancements:

  • The specific roadmap for adding ATP functionality to service quotations is not planned.

For now, the recommended approach is to use the manual availability check as a workaround when creating service quotations, and then utilize the full ATP functionality once the quotation is converted to a service order.

 

Typically you do not want that Quotations already hard-book quantities in your MRP logic, because probability that a quotation gets an actual order is below 100% and you don’t take such commitments just because you are offering a service to a customer. 
 
If the quotation would already get confirmed ATP quantities, it would take away the stock for potential service orders that get in the system second later.
 
It follows the same principle as with regular Sales Quotations, also these business objects won’t reserve quantities unless they are taken over into an actual Sales Order.
 
Best regards,
Dequan Xu

entry.title: Overcoming SAP CPI Monitoring Limitations: Retrieving Attachments via OData API
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) provides robust monitoring capabilities through its Message Processing Logs (MPL), where users can track message processing and retrieve attachments. However, a common challenge arises when the SAP CPI monitoring UI limits attachment visibility to only the first 500 attachments, even when there are thousands.

This blog post provides a detailed guide on how to bypass this limitation and retrieve all attachments using the SAP CPI OData API.

Retrieving Attachments via SAP CPI OData API

          1.Identify Your CPI Tenant URL

For Neo Environment:

https://<tenant Id>-tmn.hci.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/

For Cloud Foundry Environment:

https://<tenant Id>.<system id, e.g. it-cpi001>.cfapps.<landscape>.hana.ondemand.com/

          2.Authenticate Your API Request

SAP CPI API requests require Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0.

Using Basic Authentication, include your credentials as a base64-encoded username and password. Basic Authentication Headers:

Authorization: Basic <base64-encoded-username:password>

Accept: application/json

Or, if using Postman, you can put the username and password directly in the Authorization

If your tenant enforces OAuth 2.0, you first need to obtain an access token and use it:

Authorization: Bearer <your-access-token>

Accept: application/json

          3.Retrieve All Attachments

Use the following API request to fetch all attachments related to a specific message ID:

GET https://<your-cpi-tenant>/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs('<messageId>')/Attachments

The Message ID can be found in the SAP CPI Monitor section:

          4.View the Content of a Specific Attachment

Once you retrieve the list of attachments, each record will have an <id> field. You can use this id to download the attachment content by appending /$value to the request.

Example API Request to Download an Attachment

GET

https://<your-cpi-tenant>:<port(443)>/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs('<messageId>')/Attachments('<attachmentId>')/$value

Conclusion

While SAP CPI’s monitoring UI limits attachment visibility to the first 500 records, leveraging API capabilities allows you to retrieve and manage attachments beyond this restriction.

entry.title: Re: What is the future for Report and Statement Templates...
entry.content:
Hi, 
We have no plans to change from Jasper Report.

We do have functionality to test Statements from within the application that can be useful for Statement changes.

* Navigate to Statement Types and Select the desired Statement Type

*Select the Main Statement Template

* Select SHOW in the bottom right of the screen

* Select an existing Voucher # to test

* Click OK

A Sample Statement is created and is opened for you to review.

entry.title: How will GRC-Focused Consultancies adapt to the New SAP Partner Points System for Gold logo?
entry.content:
With the recent changes to the SAP Partner Points system, consultancies focused on Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) modules (e.g., AC, PC, RM, AM) are facing a significant challenge. The new framework no longer includes certifications related to GRC, and the points are now solely awarded for expertise in functional modules (BackOffice).

This leaves many GRC-specialized consultancies, like the one I work with, in a difficult position. It’s unclear how a consultancy focused exclusively on GRC can accumulate enough points to maintain its Gold Partner status under this new model. Does anyone have insights into how consultancies in this niche can navigate these changes? Are there plans from SAP to introduce GRC-related certifications or new ways for GRC-focused firms to earn the necessary points?

entry.title: Re: CR for VS dropped after 2027?
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

CR for VS follows the current version of Crystal Reports maintenance. If you follow that page you'll see the date gets extended every time a new release is available.

And as you know because I've posted it multiple times CR for VS only supports the current version, SP. Because SAP doesn't patch older SP's, full builds only, so if you find an issue it will get resolved in the next SP. As for the maintenance all of BI products are current version less 2 are supported.

Like all other software SAP releases patches to bring the product up to date with third party dependencies as soon as they can, some take longer than others due to implementation limitations.

Before I retired I was discussing the 32 bit version with R&D, due to ALL mother boards supporting 64 bit OS's there was no reason to install 32 bit versions of Microsoft Windows. Same as when 16 bit OS's back in the late 90's when 32 bit versions became available, yes I was supporting CR back then.... After CR 7 there was only a 32 bit version available.

So it's that time again to no longer maintain the 32 bit runtime, I believe SAP no longer even supports 32 bit versions of Windows.

As for your ERP programs, as noted in the other post, 32 bit will continue to run using the last SP that supports 32 bits but at some point you will need to move to 64 bit to keep up with supportable. I don't know how long VB 6 in compatible mode will continue to work, that's a Microsoft thing... You customers will need to upgrade.

And correct, as of December 2025 there will no longer be 32 bit runtime for distribution...

Don

entry.title: What are the steps required after changing database host name and services?
entry.content:
What are the steps required after changing database host name and services?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What are the steps required after changing database h...
entry.content:
In a scenario where you want to change database hostname and services name, you can follow the steps below as a good practice:

First of, export the repository to .atl, as described in the KBA below:

  • 2768127 - Data Services repository migration steps - SAP Data Services

After that, follow the steps below:

  1. Open Data Services Server Manager
  2. Remove from Server Manager all the information related to the old database server
  3. After removing the old information and updating with the new hostname, create a new database and export the .atl repository

entry.title: Re: How to resolve the "Save Failed" error in SAP Web Com...
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, it is not possible to identify your specific issue without more detail.  It would be:

- The user getting the error does not have authoring permissions in Manager
- There is an issue with SSO and so the user getting the error is not identified as an Enable Now author
- SAP Enable Now Manager server settings may not be setup correctly
- The SAP Companion configuration is not complete or correct

Given that it could be a whole range of things, I would start with this post.  If after going through the post you do not have an answer, perhaps raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-CMP and get the support team to take a look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SAC - Is there any way to change drill limitation in...

The short answer currently is ‘No’.

We are currently configuring all our tables in scripted stories with the setting on the table set to ‘Unlimited’. I guess if you needed say two specific limit settings, you could use two pre-configured tables and then swap them at runtime via scripting.

Hopefully this is something SAP can address along with all the other scripting deficiencies.

Cheers, Ivan.
